calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing For Petitioin For Injunctive Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling

Demurrer To Petition For Injunctive Relief And Mandatory Investigation
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing court reporter Shanna Gilbert was on Zoom. There being no appearances, the reporter is released at 10:10am.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S OFFICE Demurrer To Petition For Injunctive Relief And Mandatory Investigation.

Defendants/Respondents City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Police Department and San Francisco District Attorney's Office's Demurrer To Petition For Injunctive Relief And Mandatory Investigation is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND.

(see word minutes for full details)

Two orders are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, WRIT OF MANDATE, IN CAMERA REVIEW, AND COURT-MONITORED COMPLIANCE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing court reporter Shanna Gilbert was on Zoom. There being no appearances, reporter is released at 10:10am.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PETITION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, WRIT OF MANDATE, IN CAMERA REVIEW, AND COURT-MONITORED COMPLIANCE

Plaintiff/Petitioner Klaus Biesenbach's Motion for Hearing for Petition For Injunctive Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance is DENIED AS MOOT. As discussed in the order sustaining the demurrer, there are currently no claims upon which relief can be granted before the court in this proceeding. Should viable claims be made in the future, this order is without prejudice to a similar motion on those claims. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties).

(see word minutes for full details)

Two orders are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings On Injunctive Relief And To Reschedule The Hearing For The Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Jan-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Prior to the hearing, Klaus Biesenback appears in person in Dept. 301. Petitioner reads the tentative ruling and states he is not contesting.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KLAUS BIESENBACH's Motion To Stay Proceedings On Injunctive Relief And To Reschedule The Hearing For The Writ Of Mandamus.

Petitioner Klaus Biesenbach's motion to stay proceedings on injunctive relief and to reschedule hearing for the writ of mandate is DENIED.

On November 11, 2025, Biesenbach filed a "petition for injunctive relief and mandatory investigation" against the City and County of San Francisco and its agencies. Biesenbach requested this court issue a writ and grant an injunction to investigate an alleged pattern of serious technological abuses against him in violation of Welfare & Institutions Code section 15630. On December 6, 2024, Biesenbach filed a "Notice of Hearing for Petition for Injunctive Relief, Writ of Mandate, In Camera Review, and Court-Monitored Compliance," noticing the hearing for January 17, 2025. On December 18, 2024, the City filed a Demurrer to Petition for Injunctive Relief and Mandatory Investigation," noticing a hearing for January 17, 2025. Biesenbach now seeks to stay proceedings including his request for injunctive relief, so the court can first resolve the writ and its legal questions of "whether Respondents acted in accordance with their legal obligations." Biesenbach cites CCP 128(a)(3) as the basis for his stay request.

The court has reviewed the register of actions. Biesenbach has not shown that proceeding with the motion for the writ, though apparently on a latter day, and staying the injunctive claim(s) over the City's objection would not promote justice or judicial economy. For the time being, matters will stay on calendar as currently set.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Injunctive Relief, Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Klaus Biesenbach, 202-836-1680 kbisenbach@icloud.com Plaintiff in Pro Per
No other appearances

Proper notice of contest was not provided to the Court by Plaintiff.

Hearing is held. Discussed, Not ARGUED.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER KLAUS BIESENBACH's Petition For Injunctive Relief, Relief, Writ Of Mandate, In Camera Review, And Court-Monitored Compliance.

Denied. Petitioner's proof of service shows that respondents were not served with the required notice per Code of Civil Procedure 1005, even assuming, which is uncertain, that petitioners were properly served with the summons and petition permitting this court to exercise jurisdiction over them.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
